meta.title: Leviathan vs OpenAI Frontier — Positioning
meta.description: How Leviathan positions against OpenAI's enterprise roadmap. CDO deliberation, convergence 0.80, publish-safe.

props.title: Run any model. Prove every action. On your hardware.
props.subtitle: Leviathan vs OpenAI Frontier — we replay what they assert.
props.meta: CDO multi-agent deliberation · 5-lens panel + code validator · convergence 0.80 · publish-safe

props.content: OpenAI just shipped the stack Leviathan has been factoring against: a **context layer + agentic runtime + observability** bundle, GA on AWS Bedrock, fronted by a merged ChatGPT/Codex. That is not an endorsement — it is information about *timing*. Lev does not win the whole market. It wins the one slice OpenAI structurally cannot serve from Bedrock: **sovereignty-constrained buyers who need to replay every agent action on their own hardware.**

props.title: 🧱 Frontier = one bundle
props.content: Context layer + agentic runtime + observability, sold as a vertically integrated platform. The exact shape Lev factors into separable planes.

props.title: 🔀 ChatGPT + Codex merge
props.content: “You shouldn’t have to think about which app to use.” Single-vendor convenience as the explicit anti-configuration thesis — shipping in “a few weeks.”

props.title: ☁️ Bedrock GA + 100+ connectors
props.content: Salesforce, Databricks, Snowflake, Box, M365. Procurement friction → zero for any F500 already on an AWS EA. A real moat — concede it.

props.title: ⚡ Utility-pricing endgame
props.content: Altman: intelligence “like electricity or water.” The model stops being the differentiator — orchestration, governance, and substrate become it. That surface is Lev’s.

props.title: 💸 Cost flipped to top-2 pain
props.content: “My company spent my entire 2026 budget in Q1.” Altman’s own answer is “more models” + GPT-5.5 at ⅓ tokens — closing the cost gap vendor-side. Cost opens the room; it is not our headline.

props.label: THE WEDGE
props.title: Frontier asserts governance. Leviathan ships replay.
props.subtitle: The one slice OpenAI cannot honestly serve from Bedrock-as-managed-OpenAI

props.title: OpenAI Frontier — asserts
props.content: “Are they following your governance, your policies?” is asked as a *question*. Model vendor, policy enforcer, audit log, cloud, and agent fabric are **one company under one EA**. A sovereignty-constrained CISO cannot sign that separation-of-duty collapse.

props.title: Leviathan — ships
props.content: The LevEvent JSONL chain the buyer’s compliance team can **replay without the model vendor’s cooperation**, against a different model, with a byte-identical audit hash — on their own hardware. Governance as a shipped primitive, not a gesture.

props.label: EVIDENCE
props.title: Why we can say it
props.subtitle: Every load-bearing claim is code-backed on main as of 2026-06-05 (external-validator ledger)

props.label: 60-SECOND DEMO
props.title: What a buyer sees in one minute
props.subtitle: Every beat grounded in a shipping primitive

props.title: 1 · Run any model (~15s)
props.content:
Flip the agent model `anthropic/claude` → `openai/gpt-5.5` → `ollama/llama-4` in one config file. Three lines, no code change, identical task.

**Tag:** “Registration, not refactor.”

props.title: 2 · Prove every action (~25s)
props.content:
Agent tries `git push --force main`. The C1+C2 kernel blocks fail-closed. The LevEvent slider rewinds 30s and re-runs against a *different* model — byte-identical audit hash.

**Tag:** “Every action. Cryptographic. Replayable. Off our cloud.”

props.title: 3 · On your hardware (~20s)
props.content:
Split screen: `lev up` local vs `lev deploy --target=on-prem` to the customer VPC. Different providers, identical kernel, identical hash chain.

**Tag:** “Frontier runs on Bedrock. Leviathan runs where you say — and proves it did.”

props.label: COUNTER-NARRATIVE
props.title: Their convenience, our separation of duty

props.content: OpenAI: “You shouldn’t have to think about which app.” — Leviathan: “You shouldn’t. Your auditor should.” Single-vendor convenience and auditable separation of duty are different problems. Frontier collapses them and sells the collapse as a feature. For a sovereignty-constrained buyer, that collapse is the procurement blocker.

props.label: WHAT WAS DEBATED
props.title: Key tensions — what won, and why

rows: “Validated our thesis” is the Hadoop/OpenStack/Mesos losing read. Ship language: “compressed our window.”
Real CFO signal, but unresolved without published Lev pilot telemetry. Anecdote opens the room; sovereignty is the wedge.
“Provider-neutral SDK, registration not refactor” survives diligence. The number (“11”) dies; the contract ships.
Under a 6-month box, dev-first is more expensive and less likely to ship. Red Hat 1999, not Docker 2014.
The shipping-pillar list is more credible than the puffed-up version a buyer catches in diligence.

props.label: DISSENT PRESERVED
props.title: Minority reports
props.subtitle: Not buried — these reinstate if the 90-day gates miss

props.title: Devil’s advocate (~75% current positioning loses)
props.content: “The framework-agnostic OSS multi-provider pitch, against Frontier+Bedrock with an 800M-user funnel and cost deepening vendor consolidation, is structurally a losing wedge. ~40% the project loses entirely without verticalization in 60 days.” The verticalize call is the *concession path*, not a bull case.

props.title: OSS-GTM dissent (dev-first)
props.content: Every OSS-infra winner (Docker, K8s, Hashicorp, Supabase) took developers first and let enterprise pull. Cede the flywheel and Lev fights certs against IBM/AWS/Microsoft — unfundable. Verticalize sequences this on a 6–18mo lag; it does not abandon it. If the regulated pilot misses Day 90, this dissent is reinstated.

props.label: HONESTY GATE
props.title: Do-not-claim discipline
props.subtitle: A sophisticated buyer reads the source. Every overclaim is a deal-cost. These never ship as fact.

rows: C1+C2 fail-closed in the WASM kernel; C3–C5 are the policy-layer roadmap.
Two primitives: agentguard git-hook gates + lev-abac Rust crate (grant/expiry/revoke). Earned-extension lifecycle = roadmap.
Provider-neutral SDK contract; 3 full-fidelity SDK providers + OpenAI-compatible passthrough.
90-day commitment, gated on SOC 2, DPA, indemnity, one named regulated reference.
Re-baseline required — figure is 2.5 months stale (commit 63ed1316).
“OpenAI compressed our window.” Timing, not endorsement.

props.label: EXECUTION
props.title: The 90-day plan
props.subtitle: Make the wedge buyable. Re-deliberate if 3 of 4 Day-90 gates miss.

items.title: NORTH_STAR hygiene
items.description: Add OpenAI Frontier to the Competitive Landscape (named as threat at :37 but absent from the tracked list). Clean the overclaims per the discipline gate.

items.title: Ship Scene 3 + Compliance v0
items.description: Enterprise Trial Kit v0.1 (mid-workflow model swap) on AWS+Azure. SOC 2 Type I auditor engaged. Design-partner LOI signed (mid-cap regional bank).

items.title: Ship Scene 2 + Pilot signed
items.description: Bind lev-abac → TS invocation gate (deny before provider call) → OCSF SIEM export. SOC 2 Type I issued. Paid pilot agreement signed ($75–150k).

items.title: Ship Scene 1 + Named reference + GA
items.description: ETK v1.0 GA, all three scenes. Publicly named regulated reference with C-level quote. SOC 2 Type II audit period started.

props.title: Day-90 binary gates
props.content: 1) Signed paid-pilot LOI from a named regulated buyer (EU bank / sovereign-health / defense).  2) SOC 2 Type II path engaged with an auditor.  3) AgentGuard runtime-lease lifecycle shipped or publicly demoted.  4) “11 providers” / C3–C5 / unshipped Enterprise-tier claims rewritten across NORTH_STAR + briefs.
Govern any agent. Replay every action. On your hardware. — The runtime your auditor can replay. Convergence 0.80. Publish.
